description: §8.5 DELETE + follow-up SELECT reflects the write (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real"). removeTask(id) runs `DELETE FROM tasks WHERE id = ${id}`.run(); the real Bun.SQL engine removes the WHERE-matched row from its in-memory table. countTasks then reads `SELECT COUNT(*) AS n`.get() → {n:2} (3 seeded, 1 deleted). The stub's `.run()` is a no-op (it never mutates the seed) and its COUNT `.get()` returns the raw first seed row — so {n:2} passes ONLY on the real engine.
rationale: §8.5: `.run()` executes a write (server-escalated, §8.5.4). The real engine persists the DELETE across driven clicks within the run's single _scrml_sql instance, so the follow-up COUNT observes the row count drop 3→2. Fork B loose-infer: tasks(id INTEGER, title TEXT) from the seed rows. The stub cannot express a write-then-read (it holds no table) — empirically its COUNT `.get()` returns {id:1,title:"a"}.
